name: Asparagus with Hollandaise Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off tough ends from asparagus.
2. Wash in a large bowl of cold water to remove any sand.
3. In a large pot, bring about 4 quarts of water and salt (optional) to a boil. Lower asparagus into the boiling water so it lies flat. When water returns to a boil again, reduce heat and continue boiling slowly for about 14 minutes. Do not overcook. Drain well.
4. Hollandaise Sauce:
- In a small saucepan, heat butter until very hot but do not brown.
- Into a blender, combine egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, and Tabasco sauce. Cover the container and turn the motor to low speed.
- Slowly pour in the hot butter from the saucepan in a steady stream while the blender is running on low. When all the butter has been incorporated, turn off the motor.
- Pour the sauce over asparagus and serve immediately.
5. If the sauce thickens too much before serving, add 1 tablespoon of hot water and blend briefly.
